title: rbac — PHP attribute contracts
description: Two attributes. `#[AsRbacListener]` marks a class as a subscriber to RBAC-lifecycle events (role/permission mutation, principal role change, cache invalidation, system-role modification refused, application/guard mismatch). `#[ProvidesPermissions]` on a service provider signals the boot-time discovery loader (`PermissionCatalogueLoader`) that this module contributes to the seed permission catalogue — read from the module's `permissions.json` blueprint. NOTE: `#[RequirePermission]` and `#[RequireRole]` are FRAMEWORK primitives owned by `backend-packages/authorization/` — they are NOT re-declared here (design.md §2 D-A1 framework/business split). This file declares only the BUSINESS-tier attributes.

attributes.name: AsRbacListener

params.name: events
params.description: Which RBAC events this class reacts to. Wildcard `*` = every event under `Academorix\Rbac\Events\*`. Specific list e.g. `['RoleAssignedToPrincipal', 'RoleRevokedFromPrincipal']` subscribes to those only. Validated against the events map at boot; typos abort application boot.

params.name: queue
params.description: Queue name for asynchronous dispatch. `null` = synchronous (reserved for cache-invalidation listeners that MUST run before the response is sent). Common values: `notifications` (default), `notifications-critical` (SystemRoleModificationRefused, GuardMismatchRefused, ApplicationMismatchRefused — compliance-critical), `audit` (write-heavy compliance streams).

params.name: priority
params.description: Listener execution order. Lower runs first. Framework-shipped invalidation listeners occupy 1–19 (must run first so the cache doesn't lag the DB). Framework audit listeners occupy 20–99. User-defined listeners default to 100.

attributes.name: ProvidesPermissions

params.name: module
params.description: The module slug this provider contributes permissions for — must match the module's `module.json:name`. E.g. `invoices`, `athletes`, `federation`. The loader uses this slug to (a) locate the module's `permissions.json` blueprint and (b) namespace the permissions so the catalogue can be reconciled per-module at deploy time.

params.name: priority
params.description: Load order across providers contributing permissions. Framework providers (foundation, compliance, auth) load at priority 1–10 so their permissions exist before any domain module needs them. Domain modules default to 100.

note: The framework-tier attributes (`#[RequirePermission]`, `#[RequireRole]`, `#[RequirePermissionAny]`, `#[RequirePermissionAll]`) live in `backend-packages/authorization/` and are consumed here — they gate every route via the `rbac.enforce_permission` / `rbac.enforce_role` middleware. See `middleware.json`.
